# executorfile.com

> Learn about Executor File, an open-source tool that creates an encrypted register of all your accounts, assets, and liabilities plus printed instructions for your executor. The page explains the three-step process (write, seal, print), security design using key splitting, and what the tool deliberately excludes. The site offers company information, support resources. Primary focus includes educational content.

Generated by [LLM.txt Mastery](https://llmtxtmastery.com) on 2026-07-19. 15 pages found, 14 analyzed, 12 included. Average quality: 9/10. 1 content cluster, ~4,968 words total.

## Resources

- [Executor File: everything your executor will need, in one encrypted file](https://executorfile.com/): [product] [educational] Learn about Executor File, an open-source tool that creates an encrypted register of all your accounts, assets, and liabilities plus printed instructions for your executor. The page explains the three-step process (write, seal, print), security design using key splitting, and what the tool deliberately excludes.
- [Built to last · Executor File](https://executorfile.com/built-to-last): [tool] [educational] This page explains the design philosophy behind Executor File, a YAML-based estate register. Users learn why the tool prioritizes plain text files, open encryption standards (age), Shamir's Secret Sharing (ssss), and paper backups over hosted services.
- [Executor File FAQ: passphrases, share holders, Windows and your solicitor](https://executorfile.com/faq): [tool] [educational] Answers common questions about Executor File, a digital estate planning tool. Learn about passphrases, 2-of-3 share splitting, Windows compatibility, what solicitors need to know, and why the tool has no app or server.
- [How it works · Executor File](https://executorfile.com/how-it-works): [tool] This page explains how Executor File works—a digital estate planning tool that encrypts a plain text register using age encryption with a 2-of-3 Shamir secret sharing scheme. Users create one file, run one command to seal it, and distribute three printed paper shares to trusted individuals.
- [Why trust this · Executor File](https://executorfile.com/why-trust-this): [tool] This security transparency page explains why users should trust Executor File, a secret management tool. It provides computed script line counts (15 scripts totaling 3161 lines), details the no-backdoor design using Shamir's Secret Sharing and age encryption, lists explicit threat model boundaries showing what is and isn't defended, and includes the full SECURITY.md policy.
- [How this site makes money · Executor File](https://executorfile.com/money): [article] Users can learn how the Executor File website generates revenue, including its affiliate link policy for article recommendations, commitment to never displaying ads, and the transparency rules governing potential conflicts of interest.
- [A sample Executor File](https://executorfile.com/sample): This page displays a complete sample Executor File in YAML format, showing example entries for various asset types including bank accounts, investments, crypto wallets, subscriptions, online businesses, safe deposit boxes, liabilities, and digital properties. Users can examine the structure, field definitions, and practical guidance for creating their own estate planning documents.
- [About · Executor File](https://executorfile.com/about): [informational] [educational] Learn about Executor File, a free open-source tool that helps users create an encrypted register of their accounts, assets, and liabilities for executors. The page explains who created the tool (Jamie Watters), how to contact the developer via GitHub issues, how to report security vulnerabilities, and how to support the project.
- [For executors: opening the Executor File](https://executorfile.com/recover): [guide] [informational] This page teaches executors how to open a deceased person's encrypted estate file using age encryption and Shamir's Secret Sharing, with step-by-step terminal commands for Mac/Linux, security warnings about using trusted computers, and legal guidance about executor authority and platform-specific legacy access policies.
- [Get started: set up your Executor File in about an hour](https://executorfile.com/get-started): [guide] [informational] This page provides a step-by-step tutorial for setting up Executor File, an open-source tool that creates an encrypted estate register and printed instructions for executors. Users download a compressed folder, install two encryption tools (age and ssss), fill in a web form with their estate details, and generate sealed encrypted files and printed guidance.
- [Learn · Executor File](https://executorfile.com/learn): [guide] A learning hub offering practical guidance on digital estate management, covering topics such as locating accounts after death, what executors encounter when managing digital assets, and strategies to reduce detective work for loved ones. Articles are currently in review, with the site redirecting new visitors to introductory documentation.
- [Opening the Executor File on Windows](https://executorfile.com/recover/windows): [guide] A technical tutorial guiding Windows 10/11 users through recovering an encrypted estate file using WSL, Ubuntu, and two command-line tools (ssss for Shamir Secret Sharing and age for decryption). Includes step-by-step instructions for installing WSL, setting up Linux tools, combining cryptographic shares, and decrypting the register.

## Optional

- [llms.txt Specification](https://llmstxt.org/): Official llmstxt.org format specification
- [Executor File on GitHub](https://github.com/TheWayWithin/executor-file): The source — short, auditable shell scripts and the schema; the tool itself, MIT licensed.
- [llms-full.txt](https://executorfile.com/llms-full.txt): The same pages with their full text inlined, for agents that want everything in one fetch.
- [Privacy · Executor File](https://executorfile.com/privacy): Learn about Executor File's privacy practices, including its use of cookie-free Plausible analytics, absence of accounts or personal data collection, and the tool's local-only execution model that keeps all user data encrypted on their own device.
- [Why this exists · Executor File](https://executorfile.com/story): A personal narrative from the founder explaining why Executor File was created after experiencing the difficulty of managing a deceased father's affairs without a clear record of assets or accounts.
